notes: The set forms a coherent visual spine on the resurrection-turn thread: it opens on the empty tomb (1), shows the psalm turning from shadow to praise (2,3), reveals the risen Christ and the Hebrews citation that names Him (4,5,6,7), holds the unashamed welcome into family (8,9), and lands on the welcoming risen Christ calling the listener in (10,11,12,13,14). No theology drift: the resurrection is carried by the Hebrews citation (scene 7) and the structural psalm turn, not over-read from bare Psalm 22; the risen Christ keeps the healed scars (consistent with Heb 2's brethren who share His suffering-then-glory), and no scene severs the Trinity or claims universal salvation (the welcome is into the family of those who come to Him). Every narration beat is carried by a strong scene; hook->climax->close (empty tomb -> risen Christ among brethren -> arms open in welcome) is a clean spine. Hero #10 is the gospel-pivot risen Christ, so the cut lands on the living Christ and His invitation.
